sections.paragraph:
Isa 16:3, and Lam 2:19 כתיב [“once לֵיל Isa 21:11 in pause, like חַיִל and חֵיל”], const. לֵיל Exo 12:42 Isa 15:1 30:29 more often with ה parag. לַיְלָה* (Milêl), in pause לָ֑יְלָה, pl. לֵילֹות [Root לוּל in Thes.], m.

night. (Arab. لَيْلُ, Eth. ሌሊት፡ Syr. ܠܺܠܝܐܳ, ܠܶܠܝܐܳ id. [“In all these cognate words there is a trace of ה parag.; see the note.”] A word, beyond doubt primitive, from which the verb לִין to pass the night, for לִילּ, appears to be formed: [in Thes. לַילּ as implying obscurity, is derived from לוּלּ]). Gen 1:5, 14 Psa 19:3, and so very often; אַרְבָּעִים לַיְלָה forty nights, Gen 7:4, 12 בְּכָל־לַיְלָה all the night, Psa 6:7 בְּיְלָה אֶחָד in one night, Gen 40:5 41:11 בַּלַּיְלָה הַהוּא in that night, Gen 32:14, 22 Gen 32:22.-By night, is expressed in Hebrew by לַיְלָה Gen 14:15 Exo 13:22 (whence יֹומָם וָלַיְלָה Exo 13:21 Lev 8:35 Num 9:21, and לַיְלָה וָיֹום Isa 27:3, לַיְלָה וְיֹומָם 34:10 Jer 14:17); poet. בַּלַּיְלָה Job 24:14 Psal. 42:9 77:7 88:2 119:55 לֵילֹות Psal. 16:7 בַּלֵּילֹות Son 3:1. הַלַּיְלָה adv. to night (like הַיֹּום to day), Gen 19:5, 34 Gen 19:34. כַּלַּיְלָה as by night, Job 5:14. Figuratively used of calamity, misery, Job 35:10 Mic 3:6 Isa 21:11 (compare חשֶׁךְ).

(*) Note. לַיְ֫לה with ה parag. pr. signified by night; however, gradually it was used for the night time (die Zeit, in welcher es Nachts ift), and night itself; just as נֶגְבָּה, צָפֹונָה the region towards the south or north, for south and north (whence בַּצָּפֹונָה, בַּנֶּגְבָּה), comp. בְּיֹומָם; this termination so coalesces with this substantive that in Chaldee and Syriac it is retained as though it were radical, and it becomes י with the termination of the emphat. state. Hence-
